text:
AGREEMENT BETWEEN

           THE CITY OF SOMERVILLE

                      AND

THE SOMERVILLE POLICE EMPLOYEES ASSOCIATION



           July I, 2004- June 30, 2008
gabriel_notes: The award explicitly relies on “wages and benefits of comparable towns” and details external comparator wage/benefit data to justify the awarded 2.5%/2%/2% wage increases and limits equity adjustments.
supporting_quote:
Moreover, the evidence also demonstrates that the overall compensation (including wages and benefits) provided to Somerville Police, although not the same, is comparable to what is
provided to police officers in these other communities.
There is, therefore, no justification for any large-scale
equity adjustments to the Somerville Patrol Officer wage
increases.

text:
Bargaining Agreement between the Town of Arlington
                    and the International Association of Firefighters,
                                   AFL-CIO, Local 1297
gabriel_notes: Comparability is mentioned only once for outside details: “Any outside detail assignment outside the borders of the Town of Arlington shall be paid at that city’s or Town’s outside detail rate consistent with current practice…”.
supporting_quote: Any outside detail assignment outside the borders of the Town of Arlington shall be paid at that city’s or Town’s outside detail rate consistent with current practice at the time this provision is agreed to. Any rules governing such outside details will be determined by the city or town in which the detail is performed.
